title: Resolve LCI mesh proxy resource ambiguity
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review. spec/11-lci.md discovery advertises </mesh>;rt="proxy", but section 17.5.5 says clients can address mesh nodes directly and no special proxy resource is required. Python currently exposes /proxy when a mesh client is configured. Decide whether native LCI should use direct IPv6 only, /mesh, /proxy, or a compatibility alias set; update discovery/link-format/spec/code and Python TUI probing rules. No hardware required.
notes:
Evidence from project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review: spec/11-lci.md discovery example lists </mesh>;rt="proxy", but section 17.5.5 says the client can reach any mesh node by direct CoAP address and no special proxy resource is required. python/src/lichen/coap/resources.py defines ProxyResource and registers it at /proxy when mesh_client is configured. The Python TUI screen map now treats direct IPv6 CoAP as the preferred conceptual model and /mesh or /proxy as optional discovered capabilities only until this Bead resolves the contract.
2026-07-02 implementation evidence:
- Contract decision: direct IPv6 + CoAP routing through the local node is the authoritative LCI mesh access model. /mesh is not an LCI forward-proxy resource. /proxy is optional RFC 7252 compatibility for local transports or host stacks that cannot route mesh IPv6 directly, discovered only as </proxy>;rt="proxy" when present and using standard Proxy-Uri.
- Updated spec/11-lci.md discovery, mesh reachability, and access-control wording.
- Updated python/src/lichen/coap/resources.py docs and ProxyResource.rt="proxy" so aiocoap .well-known/core advertises the optional helper correctly.
- Updated docs/python-native-tui-support.md to clarify TUI/direct CoAP behavior and optional /proxy compatibility.
- Added tests in python/tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py and python/tests/coap/test_proxy.py to prevent </mesh>;rt="proxy" regressions, verify /proxy discovery with mesh_client, and verify /proxy is absent from discovery without mesh_client.

Explorer evidence:
- Hegel mapped spec/docs and recommended direct IPv6 authoritative, /proxy optional, no /mesh proxy; flagged access-control wording and proxy discovery verification. Addressed both.
- Meitner mapped Python client/TUI/resource surfaces; confirmed no TUI implementation change needed and focused tests initially only had a brittle line-wrap assertion. Fixed test to normalize whitespace.

Review evidence:
- Kuhn: no findings; confirmed aiocoap Resource.get_link_description consumes rt; suggested negative discovery assertion, which was added.
- Planck: no findings; confirmed contract coherent across spec/resources/docs/tests.
- Banach: no P0/P1/P2 findings; confirmed Python behavior matches discovery claim.

Validation evidence:
- Local: git diff --check passed.
- Local: cd python && uv run pytest tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py tests/coap/test_proxy.py tests/coap/test_resources.py -q -> 26 passed in 30.08s.
- Local: cd python && uv run ruff check src/lichen/coap/resources.py tests/coap/test_proxy.py tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py -> All checks passed.
- Local mypy attempted: cd python && uv run mypy src/lichen/coap/resources.py tests/coap/test_proxy.py tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py -> failed on pre-existing untyped aiocoap imports/resource base classes and existing untyped test helper calls (25 errors), not on this contract change.
- EC2 not used: task is Python/spec/docs only; no Zephyr/native_sim/qemu/Twister validation required.
